item.title: SPICY MEME DUMP (of funny S***) #531
item.description:
1. I would watch more of THIS show
2. Even trees have to pay the bills I guess
3
4. Another reason to have a dog
5. Perfectly programmed, no bugs
6. What ANIME is this?
7. Cats vs. The World
8. Cat owners: "Why do people think we're weird" Also cat owners:
9. Shake it... um, whatever this is
10. Stayed at a cabin when this weird squirrel showed up
11. Not a bad attempt
13. I feel these two are some sort of crime duo
14. Imgur transporting their mascot
15. The only game where you can brick the AI in the face Hey Imgur friendos, I hope you enjoyed this dump. I want to share with you that when I'm not making questionable posts on Imgur, I'm actually working on comedic game of my own. It will be out on Steam in less than 3 weeks (16/04/2025)! By wishlisting, you’ll be supporting important scientific research into whether absurdity has any limits (spoiler: it doesn’t). Think of it as the existential crisis you didn't know you needed, wrapped in a wacky, fourth-wall-breaking package. Your future self is already thanking you... or possibly blaming you. Steam page: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_Or_Youll_Delete_The_Multiverse/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: What Happened Today #61
item.description: They’re doing all this in plain view. From ICE deporting a U.S. resident to the mass firings of scientists to judges handing federal buildings to Musk’s agency for free, today's chaos is coordinated. Trump’s allies are purging public institutions, canceling dissent, and daring anyone to stop them. But not everyone’s rolling over. From Wisconsin voters to Cory Booker on the Senate floor, resistance is breaking through. Stay loud. Stay sharp. Stay in the fight. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://www.jsonline.com/story/news/politics/elections/2025/04/01/wisconsin-supreme-court-election-results-brad-schimel-susan-crawford/82417975007/ 2. https://www.politico.com/news/2025/04/01/patronis-fine-trump-florida-00265436 3. https://www.thebaltimorebanner.com/community/local-news/father-deported-el-salvador-ice-mistake-4SFHFQ657NDQJEQDCHVAZ7YTCA/ 4. https://apnews.com/article/trump-migrant-children-lawyers-4304ba9d06a48f808df8650ff25e4a6e 5. https://apnews.com/article/military-academies-dei-hegseth-trump-ba9731f24b4eb4bd9c02b568209f97af 6. https://abcnews.go.com/US/mahmoud-khalil-case-new-jersey/story?id=120387445 7. https://www.bostonherald.com/2025/04/01/ice-addresses-detaining-boston-defendant-outside-courthouse-mid-trial/ 8. https://www.axios.com/2025/03/11/mike-johnson-proxy-voting-discharge-petition 9. https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/trump-administration-suspends-host-federal-grants-princeton-university-rcna199127 10. https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/23-state-attorneys-general-sue-trump-administration-over-decision-to-rescind-billions-in-health-funding 11. https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2025/04/01/trump-greenland-us-territory-cost/ 12. https://federalnewsnetwork.com/workforce/2025/04/humiliating-and-degrading-hhs-employees-learn-of-layoffs-when-their-id-badges-stop-working/ 13. https://www.newsweek.com/mike-waltz-used-personal-gmail-government-communications-report-2053863 14. https://www.mediaite.com/news/doug-emhoff-urged-his-law-firm-to-fight-trumps-threats-they-bent-the-knee-anyway-report/ 15. https://www.nytimes.com/2025/04/02/us/politics/oscar-arias-sanchez-visa-trump.html 16. https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5226967-judge-extends-block-on-probationary-employee-firings-but-only-in-some-states/ 17. https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/other/ar-AA1C5s02 18. https://www.wired.com/story/judge-approves-doge-usip-office-building/ 19. https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/apr/01/cory-booker-senate-speech-trump

item.title: How do ya like my new tailgate?trans
item.description: It’s a work in progress, but I’m as done with the spray paint as I’ll ever get. Its imperfections are there to reinforce my own. I’m not trans. Maybe. An egg perhaps? I dunno. Yay therapy! But this is a WIP by an Ally. More to come. Most viral edit: NGL and say I’m not a little shaken by all the warnings, but then, I always wanted to die on a hill (FR though, I don’t want to die). Thanks for the Ups y’all. Keep on loving.

item.title: "The Feminine Mystique" - read it.
item.description: The Feminine Mystique by Betty Friedan critiques the discontent women faced in the mid-20th century, despite fulfilling domestic roles as housewives and mothers, and calls this unease the "problem that has no name." This book became a catalyst for second-wave feminism, inspiring women to challenge societal norms and pursue opportunities beyond the home. However, as the feminist movement gained traction, it faced backlash from conservative men and institutions that sought to preserve traditional gender roles, often dismissing feminist ideas as dangerous or undermining family values.

Step 4: Replace new towel and kitties

item.dc:creator: PostreOnnaEermageur

item.title: "I hate my baby" *could* mean a cruel mother, but it probably means a depressed mother instead.
item.description:
I recently came across two Reddit posts made by women that just had a child: Post 1: I do not like my baby at all. Post 2: I hate my daughter. To protect these users, I'm not going to link their posts. They made their posts to get a heavy feeling off of their chest, and this heavy feeling is often scrutinized with intense scorn. I'm not going to expose them to more angry messages by outraged people who read this post. . Anyway... For a lot of people, the idea of a mother hating their baby is incomprehensible. Women are believed to have a natural maternal instinct that kicks in immediately after giving birth. If a woman hates their baby, it's assumed that it's because they're a narcissistic abuser that can't think of anyone but themselves. However, while narcissistic parents exist, mothers feeling hatred or resentment towards their babies is actually a common occurance. "I hate my baby" is a key indicator of a post-birth complication called "postpartum depression" (PPD). There are treatment options available to make these feelings lighten up or even go away entirely. I wanted to take a moment to cover PPD to give hope for those who might be having similar feelings, AND to give a better understanding of PPD for those who aren't familar with it.
While this post is focused on women, I want to make a note that postpartum depression and anxiety can happen to fathers as well. This also applies to parents that identify as non-binary, especially if they have female reproductive systems. I am focused on mothers right now because they're the ones that both experience it more intensely, and catch the most heat for it. . Postpartum Depression in simple terms is major depressive disorder (aka "clinical depression") that has been triggered by having a baby. The effects of pregnancy and childbirth don't evaporate once the baby is born. In fact, even when the placenta comes out, the HCG hormone that it produced during pregnancy will remain in the body for several weeks after. Other hormones rise and drop dramatically that can affect a state of mind. Then there's the painful physical recovery of giving birth - whether it was a vaginal delivery or a c-section. The changes of mind mixed with physical pain are then aggravated by the demanding job of caring for a newborn - sleep deprivation, constant feeding and changing, the baby can only communicate via loud screaming, etc... In the end, you have hormonal changes + physical pain + exhaustion = a bad time. What are the symptoms of postpartum depression? I'm glad that you asked! I managed to find a couple of checklists from reliable sources that go through PPD symptoms.
I'm not sure how helpful "for no good reason" is on this particular checklist. "A good reason" for something is an opinion statement, and can lead to confusion. What is a good reason for being anxious vs. not a good reason? Not to mention that it makes someone feel judged by what should be an objective psychological evaluation. . This is basically what they're asking: "I have felt [negative feeling] either unprovoked, or more intensely than what fits the situation." . This question is really just asking you if you've had negative emotions that are either waaaayyy overboard for the situation, or they come out of nowhere without any external triggers. Ex 1: Falling into total despair when you accidentally drop a cup on the floor. Ex 2: Feeling panicky and like you're in immediate danger despite being in your livingroom.
This checklist focused more on a mother's feelings towards their baby. It is not uncommon for an exhausted and depressed mother to have intrusive thoughts about harming their baby, such as wanting to throw them out a window. It doesn't mean that you're actually going to hurt your child. It's a thought that is brought on by intense frustration that might feel difficult to control. I saw a comment on YouTube that actually stuck with me: "My midwife always said that it is totally normal to think about throwing your baby out the window. It's not normal to actually do it." They're absolutely right. There was a video excerpt from the TV Show "Chicago Med" about postpartum depression that I love a lot because it depicts PPD very well: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hHfgf3joLRE . . There is a milder form of PPD called "Baby Blues," where the effects of depression are both milder and don't last as long. I found a good comparison between the two. (See next image).
Another big difference between "Baby Blues" and PPD is that Baby Blues typically only lasts 1-2 weeks, and doesn't require extra treatment. It goes away on its own. Postpartum depression, however, can last for years, and it DOES require extra treatment like antidepressants and therapy to get it under control.
This infographic beautifully lays out ways to handle postpartum depression. If you don't have a solid support network or a partner to help with carrying the load, don't be afraid to call a crisis hotline for help. If you feel really overwhelmed and out of control, go to the ER with your baby and explain your situation. They will help you. . If you're in the US and are hesitant to go to the ER out of fear of a massive medical bill, call this hotline for help: (1-833-852-6262). . There are many different treatment options for PPD. A big one is therapy ands antidepressants, because you are severely depressed. One thing that I really want you to hold onto: PPD DOES NOT LAST FOREVER. THERE IS LIGHT AT THE END OF THIS DARK TUNNEL. WHILE IT FEELS TERRIBLE RIGHT NOW, YOU WILL SURVIVE THIS.
You are not alone in experiencing overhwhelming sadness, anxiety, anger, guilt, shame, etc... You're not a terrible parent for having dark thoughts of any kind, and it DOES get better. It's hard to hold onto hope when things look so bleak. These words you're reading might just seem like empty promises, but it's the truth. You will come out on the other side of this horrible experience. With proper treatment, PPD does get better.
I give ALL parents congratulations on their new babies, but "congratulations" typically isn't a helpful thing to say to someone who is struggling with PPD. However, I want to end on some sort of positive note. . For those that are enjoying their babies and parenthood: Congratulations on the little one! I wish your little family health and happiness! :)
National Maternal Mental Health Hotline: https://mchb.hrsa.gov/programs-impact/national-maternal-mental-health-hotline#:~:text=Espa%C3%B1ol-,You're%20not%20alone,1%2D833%2DTLC%2DMAMA Baby Blues vs. PPD: https://doh.sd.gov/topics/mch/pregnancy-early-childhood/first-1000-days/you-and-baby/postpartum-depression/#:~:text=It's%20very%20common%20for%20women,%2D800%2D944%2D4773. Mayo Clinic’s Overview of Postpartum Conditions: https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/postpartum-depression/symptoms-causes/syc-20376617#:~:text=Most%20new%20moms%20experience%20postpartum,for%20up%20to%20two%20weeks. Postpartum Depression Statistics: https://www.therecoveryvillage.com/mental-health/postpartum-depression/ppd-statistics/ Lengthy Postpartum Depression Overview: https://diamondrehabthailand.com/what-is-postpartum-depression/ How to Manage Postpartum Depression & Anxiety: https://safehugs.in/blogs/kids-and-parents/understanding-and-overcoming-postpartum-depression-a-comprehensive-guide Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (Checklist 1): https://novopsych.com/assessments/diagnosis/edinburgh-postnatal-depression-scale-epds/ Postpartum Depression Checklist 2: https://www.thebump.com/a/postpartum-depression
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: A cakeday dump of politics, kink, and punk
item.description:
Let's a go!
Haven't done it yet, but that's only cuz I'd die
"are you still watching?"
Pretty much my friend group
Seriously we should be careful about microplastics. Don't use plastic water bottles. PSA over. And now for something completely different.
Tampons happen to be extremely absorbent AND flammable. Just sayin.
Diabeetus
These are my emotional support bones
Do your part!
My band really missed the mark on naming...
But they're COOL gang signs
Legendary behavior
Never let others tell you how to be you
Hot shower, blankie, and chocolate
I volunteer
It's called flavor, sweaty, look it up
Me waiting to go on at midnight and trying so hard not to fall asleep
Fight back
*pirates of the Caribbean music starts to swell*
Nailed it.
Don't yuck other's yums
I wanna munch!
Blue eyes, white Jesus
I'm taking notes
My boys!
Why is this hard for folx to understand?
It's a me!
item.dc:creator: eddylee13

item.title: Truth
item.dc:creator: Shenaniganz80

item.title: Things seen this week during structural assessments!
item.description:
Foundation cracks wider than 1/4th of an inch are concerning as they often signal underlying structural problems.
Brick foundations are not ideal in seismic-prone areas because they can’t handle the stresses of earthquake movement. You can see this one is already showing signs of failure.
This was an attempt at temporary support. It was meant as a quick fix, but it’s been in place for years. Since homes like this rely on their perimeter foundation for proper support, this isn’t a long-term solution.
Long horizontal cracks in a foundation are a strong indicator of spalling. The exposed aggregate further indicates concrete deterioration, while the white powdery residue, known as efflorescence, points to ongoing water intrusion.
Brad shows a huge fissure in this crawlspace caused by expansive soil. This type of clay soil swells when wet and shrinks as it dries, creating constant movement. Over time, this cycle leads to shifting, cracking, and deep fissures like the one seen here.
Helical piers are steel supports that resemble large screws. They are twisted into soil to help stabilize structures but are not suitable for all soil conditions.
These helical piers were installed on this home in Portuguese Bend but failed due to the ongoing land movement. In general, helical piers are not suitable in hillside areas. Caissons are a more reliable solution, as their deep embedment and durability allow them to withstand heavy structural loads more effectively.
This was found beneath a home addition, where cinder blocks were used instead of proper piers. This is not how a post-and-pier system is meant to be built and does not provide adequate structural support. Thank you for joining us, we hope you enjoyed! Check out our Instagram for more exciting content, and we'll see you next Tuesday at 2 pm! https://www.instagram.com/alphastructural/
item.dc:creator: AlphaStructural

item.title: Does a bear dump in the woods? - 100
item.description:
Rantch
Folks, lemme tell you, we have the best pies, nobody bakes pies like we do, we're gonna do it better than they've ever done it, it's tremendous, these pies, and they, you know, people come up to me all the time, they come up to me and say "you have the best pies, how do you it?" It's true! But the Democrats, they try, oh do they ever, they want cakes, they want turnovers, and the Danishes, they're terrible, those Danishes, terrible allies, nasty bakers, and that's why we're gonna buy Greenland, the deals we're making, the pies we're baking, it's, yes, it's wonderful these pies, and we're gonna bake America great again, you'll see, believe me folks, the crusts will rise, it's gonna be yuge.
Bear fact: Upon this wretched eve, we are, all of us, fools. 099 - https://imgur.com/gallery/OGkYvLX
item.dc:creator: bearatrooper

item.title: For a trophy
item.description:
Doing it for a trophy
Qwest?
Qwest!
Qwest...
Once more from the top
Worldwide shipping has begun for Swords: Cut the Deck ~ https://swords-cut-the-deck.backerkit.com/hosted_preorders
There are only 4 God Editions left for New Zealand and Australian customers, then the shipping price will shoot up
The series is always trying to evolve into something new...
Like when we discovered there are dozens of Dwarven clans, each themed around a different metal
HG!
AU!
Zzz...
Are you sure that counts as a sword?
The real heroes of SWORDS
And their panties
Oh and they have a copy of Cut the Deck with them, neat
The real cards aren't sword-shaped, but sometimes you have to make sacrifices to meet manufacturing deadlines. It's been a real challenge.
All these God Editions went to Australia this week...
Gold
Silver
Iron
Maybe the real monster was the friends we made along the way
Oh no the real monster really was the friends we made along the way
I've probably been talking too long...
So I'll leave you with this to chew on...
Choose your weapon.
item.dc:creator: swordscomic

item.title: Do.... it....
item.dc:creator: DontYouHateWhenAllUsernamesAreAlreadyTaken

item.title: Just a couple of bites
item.description: I’ve noticed over the years that pregnant mama cats start getting…bite-y and attack-y the closer to birth they get. They revert right back to sweethearts after their babies are born, and she’s so uncomfortable right now that I can look past the aggression. She’s VERY mild right now, but I anticipate having to use cardboard boxes to cover my legs as I enter and leave the kitten room very soon. I love her anyway
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: No joke, over 300 meter volcanic fountain right now
item.description: Kilauea on Hawaiʻi island https://www.youtube.com/live/oG5zz9Sjw3E Check it out on USGS channel on YT, its getting dark here and more stunning. "Live view of the eruption in Halemaʻumaʻu, from the northwest rim of the caldera, looking east."
item.dc:creator: TripUpStairs

item.title: Sue me, a beautiful man is a beautiful man
item.dc:creator: MadameRainbowPants
